name: Peppermint Petits Fours
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the crusts from the pound cake. Cut the cake lengthwise into 4 layers.
Spread one layer with half of the preserves. Top with the second layer.
Repeat to make 2 double layers. Cut each into eight 2-inch squares (total: 16).
Set aside.
2. Combine granulated sugar, water, and cream of tartar in a 3-quart casserole.
Microwave on High for 5 to 7 minutes, or until the mixture boils. Stir.
3. Insert a microwave candy thermometer. Microwave on High for 7 to 11 minutes,
or until the mixture reaches 226°F. Let stand on the counter about 1 to 1 1/4 hours,
or until cooled to 110°F. (Do not stir or cool over water.)
4. Add powdered sugar, peppermint extract, and food coloring. Beat at medium speed
of an electric mixer until smooth.
5. Using 2 forks, dip each layer pound cake square into the frosting, coating all sides.
Let excess drip off. Place on a wire rack over wax paper. Repeat, dipping each square twice.
6. Decorate with crushed peppermint, candies, colored sugar, or strawberry slices.
Let stand until set.
